channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Reconciliation Australia marks 25 years of advancing Indigenous partnerships and reconciliation initiatives
item.description: Reconciliation Australia is celebrating 25 years since its establishment as National Reconciliation Week begins. The organisation was created in 2001 after the conclusion of the Council for Aboriginal...

item.title: The CompanY and Neocolours announce joint concert performance
item.description: ABS-CBN News reported that two well-known Filipino music groups, The CompanY and Neocolours, are set to join forces for an upcoming concert. The announcement highlights a special collaboration between...

item.title: Proposal to allow remote attendance in Senate sessions raises questions over who benefits
item.description: A proposed change in the Philippine Senate is seeking to allow senators to attend plenary sessions online under “justifiable reasons,” a move that has sparked debate over its potential implications. T...

item.title: Republican Messaging Against James Talarico Highlights Gendered and Online Culture Rhetoric in Texas Senate Race
item.description: The article examines the emerging general election battle in Texas between Democratic state representative James Talarico and Republican attorney general Ken Paxton, focusing on the tone and content o...

item.title: White House Faces Backlash Over Controversial Anti-Immigrant Video
item.description: The White House recently posted a video on its official X account that has drawn widespread criticism for its anti-immigrant content. The video, which appears to be AI-generated, depicts a UFO lifting...

item.title: PEI family launches lawsuit over PFAS contamination in well water near Hazelbrook
item.description: A family in Hazelbrook, Prince Edward Island, has filed a lawsuit against the provincial government after discovering significant contamination of their well water with per- and polyfluoroalkyl substa...

item.title: Nicholas Chavez: American Actor of Mixed Hispanic and White Heritage
item.description: Nicholas Chavez is an American actor born in Houston, Texas, on 6th September 1999. Despite many assuming he is Mexican due to his surname, he is actually of mixed ethnicity: his father is Hispanic wi...

item.title: Treasury Secretary defends proposal to feature Donald Trump on new $250 bill amid legal and political controversy
item.description: The article reports on controversy surrounding U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent’s defense of a proposal to place President Donald Trump’s portrait on a newly proposed $250 bill, which would be is...

item.title: Maddox Jolie-Pitt files to legally remove Brad Pitt’s surname amid ongoing family estrangement
item.description: Maddox Jolie-Pitt, the eldest son of actors Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie, has reportedly filed legal documents to remove “Pitt” from his surname and officially change his name to Maddox Chivan Jolie. ...

item.title: Protests and Federal Agent Clashes at Newark Immigration Detention Center
item.description: On Thursday evening in Newark, New Jersey, tensions escalated outside the Delaney Hall Detention Center as anti-ICE demonstrators clashed with federal agents. The privately operated facility, which re...

item.title: Nigerian Navy Marks 70 Years, Reports Expansion to Over 100 Vessels and Ongoing Maritime Security Efforts
item.description: The Nigerian Navy has disclosed that it has acquired more than 100 warships and boats since its establishment in 1956, marking 70 years of existence. This was revealed by Rear Adm. Ebiobowei Zipele, F...

item.title: National Assembly Backs Tinubu’s Security Reforms and Proposed State Police Framework
item.description: Senate President Godswill Akpabio say the National Assembly go continue to support President Bola Tinubu on security matters and major economic reforms wey government believe fit improve the country. ...

item.title: Addressing Youth Unemployment in Tanzania Through Strengthening the Informal Sector
item.description: Tanzania is currently dealing with a serious and growing youth unemployment challenge as more young people enter the labour market each year. The article highlights that the formal sector, including g...

item.title: Inquiry ordered into railway alignment issues and large-scale deforestation for Khajuraho–Panna rail project in Madhya Pradesh
item.description: Indian Railways has initiated a formal inquiry into the alignment of the proposed Khajuraho–Panna railway line in Madhya Pradesh after concerns were raised that the originally approved route was unsaf...

item.title: Rescue Efforts Underway for Four Climbers Who Fell Near Denali Pass
item.description: Rescue teams are attempting to reach four climbers who recently fell on Mount McKinley, also known as Denali, the highest peak in North America at 20,310 feet. The incident occurred near Denali Pass a...

item.title: IMD Forecasts Possible Delay in Kerala Monsoon Onset Due to Weak Rainfall Conditions
item.description: The southwest monsoon onset over Kerala may be delayed this year despite the India Meteorological Department (IMD) earlier predicting an early arrival around May 26. According to the latest extended r...

item.title: Former defence minister Linda Reynolds presses ahead with AUKUS amid warnings of an 'imaginary' war
item.description: This analysis argues that Linda Reynolds, the former Australian defence minister, frames her advocacy for the AUKUS security pact as if Australia faces an imminent and existential threat. The piece cr...

item.title: CBSE modified OSM tender conditions after two failed bidding rounds before nationwide Class 12 rollout
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) reportedly floated three successive tenders for its On-Screen Marking (OSM) system before successfully selecting a vendor for the nationwide Class 12 bo...

item.title: Nduduzo Makhathini reflects on collaboration, memory and intergenerational South African jazz
item.description: Nduduzo Makhathini has returned to Durban after an intense international tour spanning the United States, the United Kingdom and parts of Europe, where he performed in multiple formats ranging from hi...

item.title: FG proposes 0.5–1% oil revenue allocation to Police Trust Fund, says Akpabio
item.description: The President of the Nigerian Senate, Godswill Akpabio, has disclosed that the Federal Government is considering a policy to strengthen the Nigeria Police Force by allocating between 0.5% and 1% of re...
